title: backend lowerings --via nuxmv/spin/storm + agreement tests
manager: FSL doesn't build a world-class model checker — it compiles to three of them (nuXmv, Spin, Storm) and requires them to agree, so a verdict is trustworthy because independent tools confirmed it. The differential agreement harness is the trick: if the backends disagree, that is a bug in a lowering, caught before a wrong verdict ever ships. Compile-to-the-experts rather than reinvent-the-expert, and cross-check the experts against each other.
cs: `check --via nuxmv|spin|storm` lowerings (SMV/Promela/PRISM) from the IR; differential verdict-agreement harness; budgets/undecided first-class across all backends.
